summary: Gruber endorses Nicholas Riley's criticism of the Mac OS X Finder, calling it an embarrassment to Apple and to the legacy of the original Finder. He highlights that after three major releases and years of development, the OS X Finder remains slow, buggy, and painful to use. He also points to a screenshot of a shoddy Finder dialog box with multiple UI problems, adding his own observation that the caution icon violates Apple's own Human Interface Guidelines by being misplaced.
key_points: The OS X Finder is slow, buggy, and painful despite three major releases of development
The OS X Finder dishonors the legacy of the original Finder from classic Mac OS
A single Finder dialog box contains at least seven identifiable UI problems
Apple's own Finder violates Apple's own Human Interface Guidelines for dialog layout
money_quotes: The OS X Finder is an embarrassment, not only to Apple, but to the good name of the original Finder, from the original Macintosh through Mac OS 9.
Why, after three major releases and multiple years of development, is the OS X Finder so slow, buggy and painful to use?
key_insight: The OS X Finder is an embarrassment that fails to live up to the legacy of the original Finder and even violates Apple's own design guidelines.
